sources.citation: Forrester JV, Dick AD, McMenamin PG, Roberts F, Pearlman E. The Eye: Basic Sciences in Practice.
sources.scope: Ocular anatomy and physiology, including lens, ciliary body, and zonular apparatus.
sources.note: Standard academic reference for ocular basic science.

sources.citation: Standring S (ed.). Gray's Anatomy: The Anatomical Basis of Clinical Practice.
sources.scope: General and ocular anatomy, including the suspensory ligament of the lens.
sources.note: Canonical anatomical reference.

sources.citation: American Academy of Ophthalmology — Basic and Clinical Science Course (BCSC), Section on Fundamentals and Principles of Ophthalmology.
sources.scope: Lens anatomy, accommodation, and zonular structure in an educational clinical-science context.
sources.note: Institutional ophthalmology education reference.

sources.citation: Zonules.com — Focus Integrity Ontology and Standard (FIO/FIS v0.1).
sources.scope: Internal framework mapping anatomical structure to the focus-integrity thesis.
sources.note: Internal conceptual framework. Not a source of external scientific fact; used only to mark framework-level claims.

sources.citation: Goldstein EB, Cacciamani L. Sensation and Perception.
sources.scope: Visual perception, attention, perceptual organization, and the perceptual processing of visual input.
sources.note: Standard academic reference for sensation and perception.

sources.citation: Palmer SE. Vision Science: Photons to Phenomenology.
sources.scope: Perceptual organization, figure-ground, constancy, and the computational study of vision.
sources.note: Canonical reference bridging perception science and computational vision.

sources.citation: Szeliski R. Computer Vision: Algorithms and Applications.
sources.scope: Computer vision and machine vision methods: detection, recognition, scene understanding, and image processing.
sources.note: Standard reference for computer vision.

sources.citation: Coalition for Content Provenance and Authenticity (C2PA) — Technical Specification for content provenance.
sources.scope: Content provenance and authenticity standards relevant to image origin verification.
sources.note: Standards-body reference for provenance and media authenticity. Cited for the existence and purpose of provenance standards, not for performance claims.

sources.citation: Kandel ER, Schwartz JH, Jessell TM, Siegelbaum SA, Hudspeth AJ. Principles of Neural Science.
sources.scope: Neuroscience of the visual pathway, retina, lateral geniculate nucleus, and visual cortex.
sources.note: Standard graduate reference for neuroscience and the central visual pathway.

sources.citation: Hubel DH, Wiesel TN. Brain and Visual Perception: The Story of a 25-Year Collaboration.
sources.scope: Receptive-field organization and feature selectivity in the primary visual cortex.
sources.note: Primary-source account of orientation columns and cortical feature detection.

sources.citation: Wandell BA. Foundations of Vision.
sources.scope: Optics of the eye, retinal sampling, color, and the computational study of vision.
sources.note: Canonical vision-science reference bridging optics, retina, and perception.

sources.citation: Rodieck RW. The First Steps in Seeing.
sources.scope: Retinal structure and function, photoreceptors, and phototransduction.
sources.note: Detailed reference on the retina and the earliest stages of vision.

sources.citation: Purves D, Augustine GJ, Fitzpatrick D, et al. Neuroscience.
sources.scope: General and visual neuroscience, oculomotor control, and central visual processing.
sources.note: Widely used neuroscience reference.

sources.citation: Snowden R, Thompson P, Troscianko T. Basic Vision: An Introduction to Visual Perception.
sources.scope: Introductory visual perception: attention, organization, motion, and constancy.
sources.note: Accessible perception-science reference.

sources.citation: Bruce V, Green PR, Georgeson MA. Visual Perception: Physiology, Psychology and Ecology.
sources.scope: Perceptual physiology and psychology, including object and face perception.
sources.note: Standard reference integrating physiology and psychology of perception.

sources.citation: Marr D. Vision: A Computational Investigation into the Human Representation and Processing of Visual Information.
sources.scope: Computational theory of vision; levels of analysis from signal to representation.
sources.note: Foundational text for computational approaches to vision.

sources.citation: Goodfellow I, Bengio Y, Courville A. Deep Learning.
sources.scope: Deep learning theory and practice: representation learning, training, and generalization.
sources.note: Standard reference for deep learning methods.

sources.citation: Vaswani A, Shazeer N, Parmar N, et al. Attention Is All You Need. Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS), 2017.
sources.scope: Introduction of the transformer architecture and the self-attention mechanism.
sources.note: Peer-reviewed conference paper introducing the transformer.

sources.citation: Dosovitskiy A, Beyer L, Kolesnikov A, et al. An Image Is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale. International Conference on Learning Representations (ICLR), 2021.
sources.scope: The Vision Transformer; treating image patches as a sequence for self-attention.
sources.note: Peer-reviewed paper extending transformers to images.

sources.citation: Friston K. The free-energy principle: a unified brain theory? Nature Reviews Neuroscience, 2010.
sources.scope: The free-energy principle and active inference as accounts of perception and action.
sources.note: Peer-reviewed review article on the free-energy principle.

sources.citation: Rao RPN, Ballard DH. Predictive coding in the visual cortex: a functional interpretation of some extra-classical receptive-field effects. Nature Neuroscience, 1999.
sources.scope: The predictive-coding model of hierarchical cortical processing.
sources.note: Peer-reviewed paper formalizing predictive coding in visual cortex.

sources.citation: Russakovsky O, Deng J, Su H, et al. ImageNet Large Scale Visual Recognition Challenge. International Journal of Computer Vision, 2015.
sources.scope: Large-scale image classification benchmark and its role in modern computer vision.
sources.note: Peer-reviewed paper documenting the ImageNet benchmark.

sources.citation: Verdoliva L. Media Forensics and DeepFakes: An Overview. IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Signal Processing, 2020.
sources.scope: Survey of deepfake generation and detection, and the limits of forensic detection.
sources.note: Peer-reviewed survey of media forensics and deepfake detection.

sources.citation: Rossler A, Cozzolino D, Verdoliva L, et al. FaceForensics++: Learning to Detect Manipulated Facial Images. IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision (ICCV), 2019.
sources.scope: Benchmark dataset of authentic and manipulated facial videos for detection research.
sources.note: Peer-reviewed paper introducing a standard deepfake-detection benchmark.

sources.citation: National Eye Institute (NEI), U.S. National Institutes of Health — Public eye-health education resources.
sources.scope: Public, institutional eye-health and vision education material.
sources.note: Government health-institution reference, cited for public educational framing, not for diagnosis.

sources.citation: World Health Organization — World Report on Vision.
sources.scope: Global vision health, the scope of vision impairment, and eye-care systems.
sources.note: Institutional reference for the public-health context of vision.

sources.citation: Bishop CM. Pattern Recognition and Machine Learning.
sources.scope: Statistical pattern recognition, probabilistic models, and machine-learning foundations.
sources.note: Standard reference for the statistical foundations of machine learning.
